description_long: This is a weathered stone sculpture mounted on a square pedestal, featuring a carved relief of a standing figure on its front face. The figure appears to be a deity or guardian, with intricate details visible in the headdress and robes, and is surrounded by a slightly recessed rectangular frame. The sides of the sculpture display vertical inscriptions, likely in Japanese kanji, which are deeply etched into the stone. The rear of the pedestal is partially hollowed out, possibly due to age or damage, revealing an irregular opening. The stone surface is mottled with patches of moss and discoloration, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. The overall form is geometric and monolithic, with a strong sense of permanence and cultural significance.
description: A weathered stone sculpture of a robed figure with kanji inscriptions on the sides, mounted on a square pedestal with visible aging and moss.
description_view_0: Front view showing the carved figure in relief and the main face of the pedestal.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical kanji inscriptions and the depth of the relief carving.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a large hollowed-out section at the base and the plain back of the sculpture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with more kanji inscriptions and a partial view of the hollowed base.
attribute_subject_matter: Relief carving of a robed figure with kanji inscriptions
attribute_location_context: The sculpture appears to be installed outdoors, likely in a traditional or historical urban setting such as a shrine, temple, or park.
